value_chain_position: AI accelerator chip design + CUDA software platform — the dominant compute layer every LLM/inference workload must run on.
relative_comps: NVDA's H100/H200/Blackwell generation commands 80–90% of AI training revenue; AMD MI300X is the only credible alternative at scale but trails on software ecosystem (CUDA lock-in). Google's TPUs and Amazon Trainium are internal-only and represent lost TAM rather than a share-capture threat. NVDA's data-center revenue grew 142% YoY in the latest reported year; AMD's AI chip revenue is ~$5B annualized vs NVDA's ~$100B+ run-rate. NVDA trades at a significant premium (~40x forward earnings) fully reflecting its chokepoint status.
